document_title: AIADMK Election Manifesto - Legislative Assembly General Election 2026
extraction_scope: Only numbered manifesto points were included. Non-point narrative text such as preamble and conclusion was excluded.
hierarchy_convention: Each point includes hierarchy_down_to_top ordered from the point itself upward to subsection/section/document.

points.text: Special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- to every family to manage the tax burden and price rise: Under the administratively inefficient Vidiya DMK government led by Stalin, the prices of essential commodities have skyrocketed like poison over the past five years. Consequently, the public has been severely affected. Similarly, various taxes such as property tax, house tax, electricity charges, and water tax have been hiked. As a result, the daily financial burden on families has multiplied. Therefore, to reduce this burden on the people, a special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- will be provided to every family.

points.text: 'Amma Illam Scheme' providing houses for homeless families: Through the "Amma Illam Scheme," the government itself will purchase land and build free concrete houses for those in rural areas who do not own a house plot or a house. Similarly, for the homeless poor in urban areas, multi-story apartments will be built and provided free of cost through the "Amma Illam Scheme." Under this scheme, when Scheduled Caste people living in a joint family, or their sons/daughters get married and establish a separate household, they will be given priority and provided with constructed houses.

points.text: • Rs. 10 lakh compensation for families of Jallikattu tamers: If Jallikattu tamers unfortunately lose their lives while taming bulls during the traditional Tamil martial sport of Jallikattu, their families will be provided with Rs. 10 lakh. Similarly, Rs. 2 lakh will be provided for medical treatment to those who sustain severe injuries. • A Government subsidy of Rs. 5 lakh will be provided to enthusiastically organize Jallikattu events in places where government orders have been issued. • The online registration system currently followed for organizing Jallikattu events will be cancelled. The previously existing manual system will be reinstated.

points.text: According to Perarignar Anna's principle, "We will extend our hand for friendship, and raise our voice for rights" is the guiding mantra of the All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam. There is no denying that for India to shine as a strong superpower, the Central Government must be strong. At the same time, State Governments must also function independently with appropriate financial and administrative rights. Accordingly, it is our policy that the Central Government must provide proper financial and administrative devolution to the State Governments and this will pressed always.
